actions.name: aggregate_geo_mentions
actions.description: Aggregate LLM mention metrics by dimension.

domain.description: Target domain (no protocol/www). Provide domain or keyword.

keyword.description: Target keyword. Provide domain or keyword.

filter.description: Whether to include or exclude matches for this entity. Defaults to include.

scope.description: Where to look. Domain scopes: any, sources, search_results. Keyword scopes: any, question, answer, brand_entities, fan_out_queries.

match.description: Keyword match type. word = full-term match; partial = substring. Defaults to word.

include_subdomains.description: Include subdomains of the target domain. Defaults to false.

target.description: Up to 10 target entities, each a domain or a keyword.

allOf.description: The engine value.
AI platform to query. `google` covers Google AI Overviews; `chatgpt` covers ChatGPT (United States + English only). Defaults to google.

location.description: Search location as a country code/name, DataForSEO location code, full location name, or latitude,longitude,radius coordinate string. Defaults to us.

language.description: Search language as an ISO code or full language name. Defaults to en.

filters.description: Filter the raw mentions dataset before it is aggregated. Provide a single condition {"field","op","value"} or an {"and":[...]} / {"or":[...]} group of conditions (nest groups for mixed logic), up to 8 conditions. Operators: =, <>, <, <=, >, >=, in, not_in, like, not_like, ilike, not_ilike, match, not_match (use an array value with in / not_in). Filterable fields: ai_search_volume (monthly AI search volume); mentions (number of mentions); platform (LLM engine, e.g. chat_gpt, google); location (location name); language (language name); sources_domain (cited source domain); search_results_domain (domain in the engine's search results); brand_entities_title (brand entity title); brand_entities_category (brand entity category). Example: {"field":"ai_search_volume","op":">","value":1000}

internal_list_limit.description: Max elements per internal grouped array (source/search-result domains). Default 10.

field.description: Field to filter on. See the endpoint's list of filterable fields.

DataForSeoFilterValue.description: Comparison value: a string, number, or boolean for scalar operators, or a non-empty array for "in" / "not_in".

or.description: Sub-expressions where at least one must match.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for Aggregate LLM mention metrics by dimension.

actions.name: collect_seo_serp
actions.description: Collect organic SERP SEO evidence.

target.description: Optional domain or URL to mark in the results, such as example.com or https://example.com/page.

location.description: Search location as a country code/name, DataForSEO location code, full location name, or latitude,longitude,radius coordinate string. Defaults to us.

language.description: Search language as an ISO code or full language name. Defaults to en.

device.description: SERP device type. Defaults to desktop.

allOf.description: The os value.
Optional device operating system. Use windows/macos with desktop or android/ios with mobile.

page.description: 1-based Google result page to start from. Defaults to 1. Implemented with Google's start parameter and ranks are adjusted to global SERP positions.

depth.description: Organic result depth to crawl from the requested page. Defaults to 10. DataForSEO bills source in 10-result pages; UnifAPI bills returned billable organic records.

limit.description: Number of organic results to return, matching the limit parameter used across other UnifAPI endpoints. Maps to DataForSEO depth and is used only when depth is omitted; defaults to 10.

allOf.description: The view value.
Controls response size. summary keeps lean rank evidence, standard omits raw extras, full includes raw rich SERP context. Defaults to standard.

include_ai_overview.description: When true, ask DataForSEO to load asynchronous Google AI Overview blocks in the organic SERP when available. This can add source cost.

people_also_ask_depth.description: Optional click depth for People Also Ask expansion. Useful for SEO content-gap research and can add source cost.

include_pixel_rankings.description: When true, request pixel rectangle data for above-the-fold and visual rank analysis. This can add source cost.

width.description: Browser viewport width for pixel ranking calculations.

height.description: Browser viewport height for pixel ranking calculations.

pixel_ratio.description: Browser device pixel ratio for pixel ranking calculations.

viewport.description: Viewport settings used only with include_pixel_rankings.

google_domain.description: Optional Google domain override such as google.co.uk or google.de.

google_search_params.description: Advanced Google search URL parameters such as nfpr=1. Prefer typed fields like page and include_omitted_results when available.

include_omitted_results.description: When true, adds filter=0 to inspect Google results that may otherwise be omitted. Useful for deep rank checks.

remove_url_params.description: URL query parameters to remove from result URLs before matching, such as srsltid.

expand_related_results.description: When true, return related/sitelink-style organic results as separate organic elements instead of nesting them.

stop_at_target.description: When true and target is provided, stop crawling once the target is found. Useful for cheaper deep-rank checks, but later competitors may be omitted.

target_match.description: How stop_at_target should match the target. Defaults to with_subdomains.

target_search_mode.description: When stop_at_target is true and multiple targets are used source, controls whether any or all targets stop the crawl. Defaults to any.

target_element_types.description: SERP element types to inspect for stop_at_target matches. Defaults to all first-level URL/domain elements.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for Collect organic SERP SEO evidence.

actions.name: find_seo_backlink_competitors
actions.description: Find competitors by shared referring domains.

target.description: Domain, subdomain, or page to analyze. A domain or subdomain is specified without https:// and www. (example.com); a page is specified as an absolute URL (https://example.com/blog/).

main_domain.description: Treat the target and competitors as root domains rather than exact subdomains.

exclude_large_domains.description: Exclude very large generic domains (e.g. youtube.com, facebook.com) from results.

exclude_internal_backlinks.description: Exclude internal backlinks from the target's own subdomains. Defaults to true.

rank_scale.description: Scale for rank values: one_thousand (0-1000, default) or one_hundred (0-100).

filters.description: Filter the returned competitors. Provide a single condition {"field","op","value"} or an {"and":[...]} / {"or":[...]} group of conditions (nest groups for mixed logic), up to 8 conditions. Operators: =, <>, <, <=, >, >=, in, not_in, like, not_like, ilike, not_ilike, match, not_match (use an array value with in / not_in). Filterable fields: rank (backlink rank of the competing domain, 0-1000); intersections (referring domains shared with the target). Example: {"field":"intersections","op":">","value":20}

field.description: Field to sort by. See the endpoint's list of sortable fields.

dir.description: Sort direction: asc or desc. Defaults to desc.

order_by.description: Sort the returned competitors. Each rule is {"field","dir"} with dir asc or desc; up to 3 rules, applied in order. Sortable fields: rank, intersections.

limit.description: Maximum number of records to return. Defaults to 100.

offset.description: Number of records to skip from the start of the results.

field.description: Field to filter on. See the endpoint's list of filterable fields.

DataForSeoFilterValue.description: Comparison value: a string, number, or boolean for scalar operators, or a non-empty array for "in" / "not_in".

or.description: Sub-expressions where at least one must match.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for Find competitors by shared referring domains.

actions.name: find_seo_backlink_domain_intersection
actions.description: Find domains linking to multiple targets.

targets.description: Domains, subdomains, or pages to find common referring domains for (1-20). Order is preserved as the 1-based index in the response.

exclude_targets.description: Domains, subdomains, or pages to exclude (up to 10). Domains linking to these are dropped.

intersection_mode.description: all (default) returns domains linking to any target; partial returns only domains linking to every target.

backlinks_status_type.description: Which backlinks to count: live (found on the last check, default), all, or lost.

include_subdomains.description: Include backlinks pointing to the target's subdomains. Defaults to true.

include_indirect_links.description: Include indirect links (via redirects or canonicals) to the target. Defaults to true.

exclude_internal_backlinks.description: Exclude internal backlinks from the target's own subdomains. Defaults to true.

internal_list_limit.description: Maximum number of entries kept in each referring_links_* breakdown map. Defaults to 10.

rank_scale.description: Scale for rank values: one_thousand (0-1000, default) or one_hundred (0-100).

filters.description: Filter the intersecting domains. Prefix each metric field with the 1-based target index, e.g. 1.rank, 2.referring_domains. Provide a single condition {"field","op","value"} or an {"and":[...]} / {"or":[...]} group of conditions (nest groups for mixed logic), up to 8 conditions. Operators: =, <>, <, <=, >, >=, in, not_in, like, not_like, ilike, not_ilike, match, not_match (use an array value with in / not_in). Filterable fields: rank (backlink rank, 0-1000); backlinks (number of backlinks); backlinks_spam_score (average spam score, 0-100); referring_domains (referring domains count); referring_main_domains (referring root domains count); referring_pages (referring pages count); referring_ips (referring IPs count); referring_subnets (referring subnets count); broken_backlinks (backlinks to broken pages); broken_pages (broken pages still receiving backlinks); first_seen (ISO date the first backlink was found); lost_date (ISO date the last backlink was lost). Example: {"and":[{"field":"1.rank","op":">","value":300},{"field":"2.rank","op":">","value":300}]}

backlinks_filters.description: Filter the underlying individual backlinks before the aggregate metrics are computed. Provide a single condition {"field","op","value"} or an {"and":[...]} / {"or":[...]} group of conditions (nest groups for mixed logic), up to 8 conditions. Operators: =, <>, <, <=, >, >=, in, not_in, like, not_like, ilike, not_ilike, match, not_match (use an array value with in / not_in). actions.name: list_seo_backlink_anchors
actions.description: Get anchor texts used in backlinks to a target.

target.description: Domain, subdomain, or page to analyze. A domain or subdomain is specified without https:// and www. (example.com); a page is specified as an absolute URL (https://example.com/blog/).

backlinks_status_type.description: Which backlinks to count: live (found on the last check, default), all, or lost.

include_subdomains.description: Include backlinks pointing to the target's subdomains. Defaults to true.

include_indirect_links.description: Include indirect links (via redirects or canonicals) to the target. Defaults to true.

exclude_internal_backlinks.description: Exclude internal backlinks from the target's own subdomains. Defaults to true.

internal_list_limit.description: Maximum number of entries kept in each referring_links_* breakdown map. Defaults to 10.

rank_scale.description: Scale for rank values: one_thousand (0-1000, default) or one_hundred (0-100).

filters.description: Filter the returned anchor rows. Provide a single condition {"field","op","value"} or an {"and":[...]} / {"or":[...]} group of conditions (nest groups for mixed logic), up to 8 conditions. Operators: =, <>, <, <=, >, >=, in, not_in, like, not_like, ilike, not_ilike, match, not_match (use an array value with in / not_in). Filterable fields: anchor (anchor text); rank (backlink rank, 0-1000); backlinks (number of backlinks); backlinks_spam_score (average spam score, 0-100); referring_domains (referring domains count); referring_main_domains (referring root domains count); referring_pages (referring pages count); referring_ips (referring IPs count); referring_subnets (referring subnets count); broken_backlinks (backlinks to broken pages); broken_pages (broken pages still receiving backlinks); first_seen (ISO date the first backlink was found); lost_date (ISO date the last backlink was lost). Example: {"and":[{"field":"backlinks","op":">","value":50},{"field":"rank","op":">","value":100}]}

backlinks_filters.description: Filter the underlying individual backlinks before the aggregate metrics are computed. Provide a single condition {"field","op","value"} or an {"and":[...]} / {"or":[...]} group of conditions (nest groups for mixed logic), up to 8 conditions. Operators: =, <>, <, <=, >, >=, in, not_in, like, not_like, ilike, not_ilike, match, not_match (use an array value with in / not_in). Filterable fields: dofollow (boolean, link is dofollow); is_new (boolean, appeared since the last check); is_lost (boolean, lost since the last check); is_broken (boolean, points to a broken page); rank (referring page rank, 0-1000); page_from_rank (referring page rank, 0-1000); domain_from_rank (referring domain rank, 0-1000); backlink_spam_score (spam score of the referring page, 0-100); item_type (anchor, image, link, redirect, or canonical); anchor (anchor text); tld_from (top-level domain of the referring page); semantic_location (link location, e.g. article, footer); first_seen (ISO date the backlink was first seen); last_seen (ISO date the backlink was last seen). Example: {"and":[{"field":"dofollow","op":"=","value":true},{"field":"backlink_spam_score","op":"<","value":10}]}

field.description: Field to sort by. See the endpoint's list of sortable fields.

dir.description: Sort direction: asc or desc. Defaults to desc.

order_by.description: Sort the returned anchors. Each rule is {"field","dir"} with dir asc or desc; up to 3 rules, applied in order. Sortable fields: anchor, rank, backlinks, backlinks_spam_score, referring_domains, referring_main_domains, referring_pages, referring_ips, referring_subnets, broken_backlinks, broken_pages, first_seen, lost_date.

limit.description: Maximum number of records to return. Defaults to 100.

offset.description: Number of records to skip from the start of the results.

allOf.description: The view value.
Controls metric depth. summary keeps the headline counters; full adds nofollow variants and the referring_links_* breakdown maps. Defaults to summary.

field.description: Field to filter on. See the endpoint's list of filterable fields.

DataForSeoFilterValue.description: Comparison value: a string, number, or boolean for scalar operators, or a non-empty array for "in" / "not_in".

or.description: Sub-expressions where at least one must match.

actions.name: search_geo_mentions
actions.description: Search LLM mentions of a domain or keyword.

domain.description: Target domain (no protocol/www). Provide domain or keyword.

keyword.description: Target keyword. Provide domain or keyword.

filter.description: Whether to include or exclude matches for this entity. Defaults to include.

scope.description: Where to look. Domain scopes: any, sources, search_results. Keyword scopes: any, question, answer, brand_entities, fan_out_queries.

match.description: Keyword match type. word = full-term match; partial = substring. Defaults to word.

include_subdomains.description: Include subdomains of the target domain. Defaults to false.

target.description: Up to 10 target entities, each a domain or a keyword.

allOf.description: The engine value.
AI platform to query. `google` covers Google AI Overviews; `chatgpt` covers ChatGPT (United States + English only). Defaults to google.

location.description: Search location as a country code/name, DataForSEO location code, full location name, or latitude,longitude,radius coordinate string. Defaults to us.

language.description: Search language as an ISO code or full language name. Defaults to en.

filters.description: Filter the matched mentions. Provide a single condition {"field","op","value"} or an {"and":[...]} / {"or":[...]} group of conditions (nest groups for mixed logic), up to 8 conditions. Operators: =, <>, <, <=, >, >=, in, not_in, like, not_like, ilike, not_ilike, match, not_match (use an array value with in / not_in). Filterable fields: ai_search_volume (monthly AI search volume); platform (LLM engine, e.g. chat_gpt, google, perplexity); model (model name that produced the answer). Example: {"and":[{"field":"ai_search_volume","op":">","value":1000},{"field":"platform","op":"=","value":"chat_gpt"}]}

field.description: Field to sort by. See the endpoint's list of sortable fields.

dir.description: Sort direction: asc or desc. Defaults to desc.

order_by.description: Sort the matched mentions. Each rule is {"field","dir"} with dir asc or desc; up to 3 rules, applied in order. Sortable fields: ai_search_volume, platform, model.

limit.description: Max mentions to return. Default 100.

offset.description: Mentions to skip. Use cursor beyond 9000.

cursor.description: search_after_token from a previous response, for deep pagination.

view.description: Response size. full keeps answers, sources, and raw search results; standard drops raw search results; summary keeps only the question, volume, and cited sources. Defaults to standard.

field.description: Field to filter on. See the endpoint's list of filterable fields.

DataForSeoFilterValue.description: Comparison value: a string, number, or boolean for scalar operators, or a non-empty array for "in" / "not_in".

or.description: Sub-expressions where at least one must match.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for Search LLM mentions of a domain or keyword.

actions.name: summarize_seo_backlink_domain_pages
actions.description: Summarize backlinks for each page of a target.

target.description: Domain, subdomain, or page to analyze. A domain or subdomain is specified without https:// and www. (example.com); a page is specified as an absolute URL (https://example.com/blog/).

backlinks_status_type.description: Which backlinks to count: live (found on the last check, default), all, or lost.

include_subdomains.description: Include backlinks pointing to the target's subdomains. Defaults to true.

include_indirect_links.description: Include indirect links (via redirects or canonicals) to the target. Defaults to true.

exclude_internal_backlinks.description: Exclude internal backlinks from the target's own subdomains. Defaults to true.

internal_list_limit.description: Maximum number of entries kept in each referring_links_* breakdown map. Defaults to 10.

rank_scale.description: Scale for rank values: one_thousand (0-1000, default) or one_hundred (0-100).

filters.description: Filter the returned page summaries. Provide a single condition {"field","op","value"} or an {"and":[...]} / {"or":[...]} group of conditions (nest groups for mixed logic), up to 8 conditions. Operators: =, <>, <, <=, >, >=, in, not_in, like, not_like, ilike, not_ilike, match, not_match (use an array value with in / not_in). Filterable fields: url (page URL on the target); rank (backlink rank, 0-1000); backlinks (number of backlinks); backlinks_spam_score (average spam score, 0-100); referring_domains (referring domains count); referring_main_domains (referring root domains count); referring_pages (referring pages count); referring_ips (referring IPs count); referring_subnets (referring subnets count); broken_backlinks (backlinks to broken pages); broken_pages (broken pages still receiving backlinks); first_seen (ISO date the first backlink was found); lost_date (ISO date the last backlink was lost). Example: {"field":"referring_domains","op":">","value":5}

backlinks_filters.description: Filter the underlying individual backlinks before the aggregate metrics are computed. Provide a single condition {"field","op","value"} or an {"and":[...]} / {"or":[...]} group of conditions (nest groups for mixed logic), up to 8 conditions. Operators: =, <>, <, <=, >, >=, in, not_in, like, not_like, ilike, not_ilike, match, not_match (use an array value with in / not_in). Filterable fields: dofollow (boolean, link is dofollow); is_new (boolean, appeared since the last check); is_lost (boolean, lost since the last check); is_broken (boolean, points to a broken page); rank (referring page rank, 0-1000); page_from_rank (referring page rank, 0-1000); domain_from_rank (referring domain rank, 0-1000); backlink_spam_score (spam score of the referring page, 0-100); item_type (anchor, image, link, redirect, or canonical); anchor (anchor text); tld_from (top-level domain of the referring page); semantic_location (link location, e.g. article, footer); first_seen (ISO date the backlink was first seen); last_seen (ISO date the backlink was last seen). Example: {"and":[{"field":"dofollow","op":"=","value":true},{"field":"backlink_spam_score","op":"<","value":10}]}

field.description: Field to sort by. See the endpoint's list of sortable fields.

dir.description: Sort direction: asc or desc. Defaults to desc.

order_by.description: Sort the returned page summaries. Each rule is {"field","dir"} with dir asc or desc; up to 3 rules, applied in order. Sortable fields: url, rank, backlinks, backlinks_spam_score, referring_domains, referring_main_domains, referring_pages, referring_ips, referring_subnets, broken_backlinks, broken_pages, first_seen, lost_date.

limit.description: Maximum number of records to return. Defaults to 100.

offset.description: Number of records to skip from the start of the results.

allOf.description: The view value.
Controls metric depth. summary keeps the headline counters; full adds nofollow variants and the referring_links_* breakdown maps. Defaults to summary.

field.description: Field to filter on. See the endpoint's list of filterable fields.

DataForSeoFilterValue.description: Comparison value: a string, number, or boolean for scalar operators, or a non-empty array for "in" / "not_in".

or.description: Sub-expressions where at least one must match.

inputSchema.description: The input payload for Summarize backlinks for each page of a target.
